intro: Ischaemum imberbe is a species of grass native to Australia, commonly referred to as the "Hill Grass" or "Blue Grass". The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ischaemum imberbe include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
